title: Failure Fingerprint Schema
description: Schema for normalized failure fingerprints used within a run and promoted into cross-run operational memory. This shape is derived from canonical-architecture.md Sections 18 and 20 plus IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Sections 1 and 5.1. Open question: canonical-architecture.md names phase, command, error signature, and relevant paths as normalization inputs, but it does not yet define a canonical field for raw stderr or other low-level payloads, so this schema keeps the normalized summary surface only.

fingerprint.description: Normalized fingerprint identifier used for deduplication and retry detection, from canonical-architecture.md Sections 18 and 19 and IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 5.1.

phase.description: Phase where the failure occurred, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md fingerprint normalization guidance.

command.description: Command associated with the failure, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 1 fingerprint normalization guidance.

error_signature.description: Normalized error signature used to group equivalent failures, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 1 fingerprint normalization guidance.

relevant_paths.description: Relevant file or artifact paths used during normalization, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 1 fingerprint normalization guidance.

type.description: Failure class from canonical-architecture.md Section 18 "Failure Handling".

evidence_refs.description: Artifact paths that support the fingerprint, following the record_failure_signature example in canonical-architecture.md Section 3.2.

root_cause.description: Root cause from diagnosis if available, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 5.1.

resolution.description: Successful resolution summary if available, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 5.1.

last_seen.description: Last-seen date recorded in cross-run memory, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 5.1.

occurrence_count.description: Total occurrence count recorded in cross-run memory, from IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md Phase 5.1.

examples.error_signature: save button disabled after valid input
examples.root_cause: SettingsForm validation state never transitions to valid after input change.
examples.resolution: Update validation state handling and add a regression test for the enabled button path.
